evals.prompt: I have a plain webforJ app (no Spring) and I need to add a small REST endpoint at `/api/health` that returns `OK` as plain text. Wire it up so the webforJ UI keeps working as before.
evals.expected_output: The agent implements an `HttpServlet` from `jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServlet`, declares it in `src/main/webapp/WEB-INF/web.xml`, and registers it in `webforj.conf` under `servlets:` so `WebforjServlet` proxies requests to `/api/health`. OR, equivalently, remaps `WebforjServlet` to `/ui/*` in `web.xml` and lets the servlet container route `/api/health` directly. Either documented approach is acceptable. The webforJ UI continues to render after the change.
expectations: Implements a class that extends `jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServlet` (NOT `javax.servlet.http.HttpServlet`)

DISCRIMINATING: AT LEAST ONE of the two valid plain-webforJ approaches is fully wired so `/api/health` is actually reachable. EITHER `web.xml` has `WebforjServlet` mapped to a non-`/*` sub-path (e.g. `/ui/*`) AND the custom servlet declared with `<url-pattern>/api/health</url-pattern>`, OR `web.xml` has `WebforjServlet` on `/*` AND `webforj.conf` has a `webforj.servlets = [ ... ]` entry whose `className` matches the custom servlet's FQCN. The assertion FAILS if neither leg holds (e.g. the agent invented a non-existent property like `webforj.servlets.exclude` and stopped there).
If the chosen approach is REMAP: `WebforjServlet`'s `<url-pattern>` in `web.xml` is a sub-path, NOT `/*`

If the chosen approach is PROXY: `webforj.conf` contains `webforj.servlets = [ { className = "...", name = "..." } ]` (the modern HOCON keys, NOT the legacy `class:` shorthand and NOT a top-level `servlets:` outside the `webforj.` namespace)
The custom servlet's `<servlet-class>` (in web.xml) and the `className` (in webforj.conf, if proxy approach) reference the SAME fully-qualified class name
Does NOT modify `WebforjServlet`'s class name or replace `WebforjServlet` with a custom class, the framework servlet stays as `com.webforj.servlet.WebforjServlet`
Does NOT introduce Spring annotations (`@RestController`, `@SpringBootApplication`, `@Bean`) or Spring-only files (`application.properties`) for this plain-webforJ scenario

evals.prompt: My webforJ Spring Boot app needs to expose a webhook endpoint at `/webhook/github` that accepts POST requests. Add the endpoint without breaking the existing webforJ UI.
evals.expected_output: Since this is a Spring Boot app, the agent uses standard Spring mechanisms to host the new endpoint (a `@RestController` mapped to `/webhook/github`, or a `ServletRegistrationBean`) AND adds `webforj.exclude-urls=/webhook/**` to `application.properties` so the URL pattern is forwarded to Spring MVC instead of being consumed by `WebforjServlet`. webforJ stays at `/*` and the existing UI URLs do not change.
expectations: LOAD-BEARING: `src/main/resources/application.properties` contains `webforj.exclude-urls=` with a value whose Ant pattern matches `/webhook/github` (e.g. `webforj.exclude-urls=/webhook/**` or `webforj.exclude-urls=/webhook/github`). Without this line, `WebforjServlet` consumes the `/webhook/github` URL at `/*` and the controller is never reached, so the feature does not work even if the controller is correctly written. This assertion is the single most important check for this eval.

Implements the endpoint using a standard Spring mechanism (a `@RestController` mapped to `/webhook/github`, OR a `ServletRegistrationBean` registering a `jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServlet` at `/webhook/github`)
If the agent writes a raw servlet class, it extends `jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServlet` (NOT `javax.servlet.http.HttpServlet`)
Does NOT edit a `web.xml` for this Spring scenario, Spring Boot apps do not use `web.xml`
Does NOT add a `webforj.conf` `webforj.servlets` entry, the proxy approach is for plain webforJ only, not Spring
Does NOT change the webforJ servlet mapping (does NOT add `webforj.servlet-mapping=/ui/*` or any other remap), webforJ stays at `/*` and UI URLs do not move
Does NOT remove or rename `WebforjServlet`, the framework servlet stays in place

evals.prompt: I want to keep `WebforjServlet` on `/*` (URLs already in production), but I also need a small servlet at `/api/echo` that returns whatever was posted. This is a plain webforJ project. Wire it up.
evals.expected_output: The agent uses the proxy approach (Approach 3 in the skill): keeps `WebforjServlet` mapped to `/*` in `web.xml`, declares the custom servlet in `web.xml`, and registers it in `webforj.conf` under `webforj.servlets` so `WebforjServlet` proxies `/api/echo` to it. The custom servlet extends `jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServlet`.
expectations: Keeps `WebforjServlet`'s URL pattern as `/*` in `web.xml` (does NOT remap it)
Declares the custom servlet in `web.xml` with its own `<servlet>` and `<servlet-mapping>` blocks at `/api/echo`

LOAD-BEARING: `webforj.conf` contains a `webforj.servlets = [ ... ]` entry with HOCON keys `className` (FQCN of the custom servlet) and `name`, optional `config`. Specifically NOT `class` (the legacy shorthand), and NOT a top-level `servlets:` block outside the `webforj.` namespace. Without these exact keys the proxy is not wired and `/api/echo` is unreachable.
The `className` value in `webforj.conf` exactly matches the `<servlet-class>` value in `web.xml`
The custom servlet extends `jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServlet`
Does NOT remap `WebforjServlet` to a sub-path, the constraint says to keep it on `/*`
Does NOT skip the `webforj.conf` registration, without it `WebforjServlet` will not proxy to the custom servlet and the URL is unreachable
